concerns.text: T014 (Checksums) is scheduled AFTER T016 (Filtering). FR-010 and Constitution Principle III require checksumming 'downloaded datasets' (raw data) to ensure data hygiene before any transformation or filtering occurs. Checksumming the filtered subset violates the 'raw data preserved' principle and breaks the data-flow dependency where the raw artifact must be verified before it is consumed by the filter.

T015 (Imputation/Exclusion) and T016 (Shapiro-Wilk/Filtering) are marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, T015 modifies the dataset (imputation) and T016 reads the dataset to filter. If T016 runs on the raw data while T015 is writing the imputed version, or if they run on different file handles without explicit locking/sequencing, this creates a race condition. The tasks share the same input file (`data/raw/*.csv`) and output to the same metadata file (`data/datasets.csv`), making them NOT parallel-safe.

T022 (Apply Transformations) lists a dependency on T017 (Metadata write). However, the actual data dependency is on the *filtered* dataset produced by T016. T017 only writes metadata. If T022 runs after T017 but before T016 completes, it will attempt to transform raw (unfiltered) data, violating the data-flow logic that transformations must only apply to valid, non-normal datasets.

T032 (Apply Transformations to Simulated Data) depends on T031 (Generate Simulated Data). T031 is described as writing a 'batch to disk'. T032 must wait for the *entire* batch write to complete before starting. The current task description 'after T031 writes batch to disk' is ambiguous; if T032 starts while T031 is still writing partial files, it will crash or process incomplete data. This requires a strict sequential ordering or a 'batch complete' signal, not just a task dependency on the start of T031.
